text:
BOTTOM LINE — Risk-on, AI chips ripped, energy cracked. SPY gained 0.66% while IWM jumped 1.89% and QQQ added 1.78%, as AI-linked semis and mega-cap tech extended leadership into a quiet, data-waiting session.[1] The tape expressed a clear pro-cyclical, AI-driven grab with DRAM up 14.56% and SOXX up 6.10% while Energy 7 fell 2.79% and XLE dropped 2.76%, showing investors funding chips by dumping defensives and energy.[1] Cross-asset moves with SLV up 1.99%, USO down 2.78%, and VIXY down 3.11% confirm a moderate risk-on regime despite elevated index put protection with SPY PCR_vol at 2.04 and QQQ PCR_vol at 2.19.[1]

INDICES — VIX printed 16.6, below its 20-day moving average of 17.6, signaling eased equity vol.[1] By 1d return: IWM +1.89%, QQQ +1.78%, IJR +1.59%, IJH +1.56%, SPY +0.66%, RSP +0.51%, GLD +0.04%, DIA -0.17%.[1] STYLE shows SMALL > LARGE by 0.11pp, and the standout cross-asset move was USO -2.78% against SLV +1.99%, highlighting a sharp energy-metal divergence.[1]

BREADTH — pct_above_sma20 was 60.6%, pct_above_sma50 was 62.5%, and pct_above_sma200 was 67.8%, while adv/dec was 125/83 with pct_up at 60.1% and uvol/dvol at 1.29.[1] Mag7 median 1d was -0.16% versus ex-Mag7 median 1d at +0.47%, narrow_leadership=False, and SPY +0.66% versus universe median +0.41% did not breach the 0.3pp divergence threshold, consistent with mega_cap_tilt=False and reasonably healthy internals.[1]

VOLUME — SPY volume ran at 0.92x implicitly via the indices average of 0.92x while QQQ volume is proxied by the elevated sector and industries activity around XLK 1.1x and SOXX 6.10% with XHE at 4.1x.[1] IJR at 1.5x and RSP at 1.0x with IWM at 0.9x show small-cap accumulation alongside XLK 1.1x and XLE 1.0x, while losing volume in GLD 0.8x and SLV 0.7x suggests today’s equity strength came with selective, but not blowout, conviction.[1]

SECTORS — XLK Technology +2.63%, XLI Industrials +1.47%, XLB Materials +1.39%, XLRE Real Estate +0.34%, XLY Discretionary +0.23%, XLC Comms +0.08%, XLU Utilities -0.04%, XLF Financials -0.17%, XLV Healthcare -0.92%, XLP Staples -1.38%, XLE Energy -2.76%.[1] Industry winners were DRAM +14.56% and SOXX +6.10%, while laggards were XOP -3.40% and FCG -3.69%, underscoring semis strength versus upstream energy weakness.[1]

ROTATION — Money-in hit AI semis and cyclicals, with DRAM +14.56% and SOXX +6.10% while XME gained 4.77%, and money-out hit defensives and energy, with XLP -1.38%, XLV -0.92%, XLE -2.76%, and Energy 7 -2.79%.[1] Defensives_minus_cyclicals at -2.39pp confirms a pronounced cyclical lean, so today’s label is broad risk-on.[1]

THE 7s — Top baskets: Fab 7 +6.74%, Semi 7 +4.02%, New Metals 7 +3.06%, Old Metals 7 +2.51%, Buildout 7 +2.47%.[1] Bottom baskets: Old Staples 7 -1.79%, Software 7 -2.03%, Health 7 -1.26%, Toll Road 7 -1.11%, Energy 7 -2.79%, with Semi 7 strength likely led by MU and AMD and Energy 7 weakness tied to XLE and XOP components.[1]

FED & LIQUIDITY — Next FOMC is June 17, 2026 with CME probabilities at hold 100%, cut 0%, hike 0%, and the stance tally shows 6 hawks, 5 neutral, and 4 doves.[1] USD liquidity stress scored 42/100 (Normal) with CP−Tbill spread at the 86th percentile as the most-stressed component, TED spread at 0.09%, ON-RRP at $1.8B, and TGA at $781,293M after a -$57,291M change.[1]

POSITIONING — SPY PCR_vol at 2.04 and QQQ PCR_vol at 2.19 with positive skew of +10.1% and +14.0% show heavy downside protection and rich puts despite the rally, while IWM PCR_vol at 1.13 indicates more balanced small-cap positioning.[1] VIX at 16.6 below its 20-day moving average of 17.6 and MOVE at 78.4 above its 20-day moving average of 74.6 signal calm equity vol but still-elevated bond vol stress that could reprice risk assets.[1]

CROSS-ASSET — USO -2.78% alongside XLE -2.76% and Energy 7 -2.79% while HYG rose 0.34% and SLV gained 1.99% points to an idiosyncratic energy unwind rather than broad credit stress.[1] The modest UUP move at -0.07% with TLT +0.50% and IEF +0.43% suggests falling yields supported growth and AI exposures more than they hurt cyclicals ex-energy.[1]

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed at 55.9/100 sits in Neutral territory.[1] Top individual movers included MU about +20%, DRAM +14.56%, and SOXX +6.10%, while 20d momentum is strongest in XLK +17.29% and Semi 7 +30.54% and weakest in XLU -1.99% and Software 7 -22.33%, with vol spikes in BLOK 2.5x and XHE 4.1x hinting at accumulation in crypto-adjacent and speculative healthcare names.[1]

WATCH — MU surged roughly 20% today with DRAM +14.56% and Semi 7 +4.02%, warranting focus for potential exhaustion or continuation in AI memory beta.[1] XLE fell 2.76% with Energy 7 -2.79% and USO -2.78%, making any follow-through crucial for gauging whether this is a one-off flush or the start of a broader energy factor unwind.[1]
